mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the PAYEMS drop, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in residential construction starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a broad-based demand contraction, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both HOUST and RSAFS decline concurrently, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects capacity expansion and supply-side resilience, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during supply-chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate addressable market for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically boosting forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the rolling Spearman rank correlation remains below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp spike in the consumer price index triggers anticipatory Federal Reserve hawkishness, compressing equity risk premiums and depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat following CPI spikes above the threshold, or forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant negative drift relative to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong total nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market friction and structural mismatch, creating earnings uncertainty that weighs on S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or the interaction effect disappears in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth coupled with robust housing starts signals early-cycle economic acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive month-over-month momentum, or the joint signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in walk-forward testing.
